explanation: This LTL property ensures that whenever the sensor data is not good (i.e., `!good` is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state (`state_is_NOMINAL` -> `state_is_MANEUVER`).  The property uses the 'until' operator (U) to specify that the autopilot must remain in the NOMINAL state (`state_is_NOMINAL`) until the MANEUVER state is reached (`state_is_MANEUVER`).  The property also uses the 'globally' operator (G) to ensure that this condition holds at all times.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the sensor data is not good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state.  It uses the "good" proposition to represent the sensor data being good. The "until" operator ensures that the autopilot state becomes MANEUVER before the sensor data becomes good again.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is not good. The property states that if the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL) and the sensor data is not good (not good), then eventually the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER). This ensures that the autopilot reacts appropriately to faulty sensor data by transitioning to a safe state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good. It uses the "until" operator (U) to express that the autopilot will remain in the NOMINAL state until the sensor data is not good, at which point it will transition to the MANEUVER state. The atomic proposition "good" represents the sensor data being good, so the negation of "good" indicates that the sensor data is not good. The atomic proposition "state_is_NOMINAL" indicates that the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, and "state_is_MANEUVER" indicates that the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state.

The LTL property ensures that the autopilot transitions from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good. It does so by stating that whenever the sensor data is not good ('!good'), the autopilot will eventually enter the MANEUVER state ('state_is_MANEUVER') after being in the NOMINAL state ('state_is_NOMINAL'). This ensures that the autopilot reacts appropriately to faulty sensor data by transitioning to the MANEUVER state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the sensor data is not good (meaning the 'good' proposition is false), the autopilot must eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state. The property ensures that the autopilot will eventually change states, but it doesn't specify when or how many times this transition must occur. This property only requires that the autopilot moves to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good, which is indicated by the atomic proposition "good" being false. The formula uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot should remain in the NOMINAL state until the sensor data is not good. Once the sensor data is not good, the autopilot should transition to the MANEUVER state, as indicated by the atomic proposition "state_is_MANEUVER" becoming true.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the sensor data is not good (indicated by "!good"), the autopilot must eventually change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER. This property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the temporal relationship between the sensor data condition and the state change. Specifically, it states that "!good" must hold until "state_is_MANEUVER" becomes true. This ensures that the autopilot transitions to the MANEUVER state only after the sensor data becomes faulty.

It uses the atomic proposition "good" to represent the sensor data quality and "state_is_NOMINAL" and "state_is_MANEUVER" to represent the autopilot's states. The formula states that whenever the sensor data is not good (negation of "good"), eventually the autopilot should be in the MANEUVER state ("state_is_MANEUVER"). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER upon detecting faulty sensor data. This formula is preferred over previous formulas because it explicitly captures the requirement of changing states based on the sensor data quality.
